reviews.title: Cheap Buys Cheap
reviews.text: Not sure what I expected for so little, so shame on me. I'll tell you these made my 9-year-old's ears hurt. I'm not sure how they could cancel any noise, as there is no buffer. I tried the ones she put on and they are very uncomfortable. Returning.

reviews.title: Great product and great price.
reviews.text: We bought a pack of these for my son because he goes through headphones crazy fast; since we bought these, he's still on the first pair. We will be buying more in the future if we have to.

reviews.title: They are priced low enough that a defective or marginal one doesn't sour the price.
reviews.text: Quality control could be a little better, but the price for the multipack is tough to beat.

reviews.title: For the cost… I am super happy with them.
reviews.text: I’m giving these earbuds five stars because they exceed my expectations when they only cost seventy cents each. I find it hard to complain about these, especially considering the cost. The sound is quite adequate for such earbuds. I use these in a classroom setting for kids who either cannot afford earbuds or forget there is at home. They are individually packaged. Nice assortment of colors. Though they are being sold by a US company, I do not show where they are made. I can only assume they are made in China.

reviews.title: Great Sound Just Wish they had a Microphone
reviews.text: Fun colors and love this type of ear plug over the cone shaped style. Listening to music on my phone sounds great  but I wish they had a microphone so I could easily answer calls. Now I have to unplug the earphones when the phone starts to ring and then re-plug after I'm done on the call. I guess they are really designed for use on exclusive music players. The listing is confusing because it says "Bluetooth" which they don't have, but the wire is about 4ft. long so your music unit can easily be kept anywhere convenient. No volume control either so that has to be handled on your phone/music player.

Buy them if you want to class up your 'phones. I did!

reviews.title: These saved my ears! Literally.
reviews.text: My new Bose 700 headphones kept causing an allergic reaction to my ears and the skin all the way around my ears.  Like blisters, redness, swelling and SEVERE itching.  The first time, I wore them for an afternoon and it took 3 weeks for the allergy to be cleared up.  I then washed the cups of the headphones with a soapy wash rag and rinsed well.  The second time I wore them for just under 2 hours and again had a reaction that took 2 weeks to clear up.  I use another brand of headphones daily to watch TV (that I also purchased from amazon) and have never had a reaction before.  I found these covers and they have completely fixed the reaction issue.  I also ordered another brand of ear cup covers off amazon to try but prefer these.  I'll be ordering another so I'll have extras just in case 😉.

reviews.title: Does what i expect it to do
reviews.text:
I expect it to keep the sweat off the ear pads, and it does this.

But since it wraps over the ear cups, it does not allow the ear cups to rest nicely around your ears.
This means that it affects the headset's noise cancellation a bit.

Also, if you wear it for a prolonged amount of time, it hurts the ears and is less comfortable. I wear glasses and this exacerbates the tension behind my ears.
This is particularly for me with my glasses.

reviews.title: Reasonably comfy, but poor fabric quality and small sound hole.
reviews.text:
06/22/22. Revised rating down to 1-star. The fabric is awful, pills badly and attracts every bit of dust and hair. I stopped using these on Zoom calls because they look disgusting. See photo. This is what yours will look like after a few months.

----------------------

Fits my Yamaha MT5 headphones fine. It may make zero difference in sound, but I feel like the center hole is about half the size it should be. Not sure what they were thinking. I'd like to see to somebody make something similar, with thicker, more breathable fabric, and 1-inch hole.

reviews.title: Very big, but useful.
reviews.text: Very large, I was hoping for a snug fit. These extended further than the actual cushion.  So if you want to charge you will need to take off or just pull off a little. Still useful as I was tired of white peel off all over my skin and clothes. But definitely recommend for a bigger headphone.

reviews.title: Difficult to connect. Oddly LOW VOLUME and INTERFERES with apps like VIBER and DUO. Easy to loose.
reviews.text:
Where do I start:::
Connecting, if you want to use it with multiple devices, you can just forget it (if connected to one device, they will not allow you to connect to another device until you actually delete the connection from the current device even if it's off, did not have this issue with Apple Pods).
SOUND LEVEL is oddly LOW. Seriously, even at max setting does not even come close to even cheap plug-in buds, so can't use them in any loud place. Noise canceling does not help with that.
If used with call apps like Viber or Duo you'll be missing every other word. Don't know how or why, but connection signal goes to crap while these are on. As soon as I switch to plug-in buds, everything works just fine.
TOO DAMN SMALL. Sure it's convenient for the ear, but trying to handle them like putting in or taking out, better be sure to do it somewhere safe in case you drop them (not outside for sure). It would be better if they had over the year clips or something.
COLLECTS all your EAR-WAX. Yes, these things go in so deep that it just gets absolutely gross (never had this problem with other buds and I clean my ears every day)

reviews.title: Nice, just don't like having to remove them to charge it

reviews.title: Solves AirPod Issue
reviews.text: I have a love/hate relationship with my AirPods and these are making me love them a LOT more. On their own, my AirPods cause my ears to hurt terribly. Not to mention, they fall out at the slightest movement, like smiling or turning my head. Talk about ANNOYING. These tips cure those ailments. Now I can exercise with my AirPods in and never worry about them falling out. I have tiny ears, and the fit is great with no discomfort at all. I love that the little hook sticks up into the cartilage of my ear to make a really secure fit. The silicon keeps the buds from irritating my ears too. I’m so happy I got these. Now I can actually put my AirPods to good use.

reviews.title: I'm not keen on the cover material
reviews.text:
While the Arae Electronic Organizer does what it says, its implementation isn't great.

The double layers of this product has one and a half flap of elastic bands to hold bundled up wires, that's 18 spots total. The rest are two large pockets and a small pocket. That's plenty of room to carry most charging accessories that you'd have with you if you were equipped with tablets and phones. For laptops and cameras, you'll need other solutions. Even action camera chargers may have a hard time fitting in this slim product.

It's not a problem that this thing is pretty much made out of nylon from the outside in but the material that makes up the exterior of this product makes it look cheap. It's a rough, synthetic material that just makes it a bit cheap looking. I think cloth or leather would have been a better choice.

In addition, the zipper sticks, making it hard to quickly open this up to retrieve your wires for charging.

reviews.title: Case closed
reviews.text:
It's pretty amazing how much this will hold and still close up properly. I am using it for many cables and some adapters, and I am also keeping those little instruction manuals that come with everything in one of the zipper pouches. You can keep a lot of cables and adapters in this, and it will still zip up and be small enough for travel.
I like this case a lot.

reviews.title: Smaller than expected
reviews.text: Water proof? Not going to try that. This is super slim so you can't really fit a whole lot in. Wires, maybe... but devices, like portable routers, not exactly. Slim portable chargers, sure. Higher capacity batteries, nope. Won't fit anything more than two iPhones thick.

reviews.title: No More Ziplock Bags
reviews.text: This is such a better alternative to tossing cables into a ziplock bag.  The nice bag with zipper allows me to keep everything in one place.  I wished the compartments were a little wider, but it's easy to just use two compartments. Nice quality bag, once I get spares I would just pack these with a spare ready and be ready to go anytime.  Nice organization addition to my travel gear.

reviews.title: Handy little case
reviews.text: Does a good job at cutting down on cable clutter. I took a black Sharpie and identified the various cords by writing abbreviations on the cloth stretch band that secures the cords. It also helps organize miscellaneous adapters and charging units. Not sure how long it will last but for organizing cords and small electronic items it’s a good buy.

reviews.title: [review for non ANC version] Pretty good deal for it's money
reviews.text:
I lost accurate track of time, but it was definitely more than 60hr of battery life. very impressive, a little scary to carry this much power on my head, but logically I understand that risks of catching fire or batteries exploding is negligible)

* sound quality - awesome (in terms of sound itself)
* build and material quality - impressive
* bluetooth connectivity - awesome. range, stability, reconnect speed, sound quality (in terms of losses via bluetooth while in varying range of "hotspot with music").
* battery quality - a little bit over a week of daily uses for 8hr+ a day. just perfect. That will be enough for a business trip out of town for a couple of days and back without need to recharge and sometimes even without prior notice to recharge before I go.
* distinct controls (bumps with space between them) - awesome. easy to use, easy to locate, no mistakes)
* folding capabilities - they do fold/unfold, but I tried it only once upon unpacking, but I rarely use this feature

The only minus that I can see in the last 20 days is how the headband is stretching when I'm wearing headphones. Looks dangerous (in terms of longevity). That's all I think. If this headphones will last more than a year I would call them perfect for a price and capabilities (tiny note about non ANC version and that's it)

So final score (while they last) 10/10

reviews.title: It is not passive soundproofing it is active. Which is NOT what I need.
reviews.text:
I have difficulty finding headphones with passive noise canceling for whatever reason and so I saw that this listing said passive and it was from the same brand as one I am still using. (It's breaking down due to age)

Active soundproofing is extremely detrimental to someone with audio disorders like myself since it makes a low buzzing noise that I can't tune out.

It does work as headphones but I would like to have it refunded so I can get another pair with the format of soundproofing I need.

reviews.title: AWESOME HEADPHONES!!
reviews.text: AWESOME headphones for music or phone calls!! Pairs up with my phone, usually within seconds. I listen to tons of YouTube music videos with these and the sound rivals more expensive sets. For the price on these you just can't go wrong!! Battery life is amazing too!!!

reviews.title: There is absolutely no bass, highs screeching high and nothing inbetween
reviews.text: These do not fit well and hard to keep in ear. My much cheaper earphones sound much better and much more bass. The treble is very high frequency but that is all you hear with this. Buy name brand and they would have more honest advertisement and better quality. I didn't expect to good bass for in ear earphones but this has non whatsoever and the high frequency could ruin your hearing. Maybe after few hundred hours it will sound better so will let you know. In the mean time pay a little more but get name brand.

reviews.title: Great sound, but a little bulky for an IEM
reviews.text: There are 6 drivers in here-1 DD and 5 BA, but the housings are pretty big. Definitely deep and will probably stick out of your ears, but sound great (with upgraded cable). For what you are paying, these IEMs are a steal. Sound fantastic. Find the the tips that work for you and upgrade the cable (I bought the 2.5 and 3.5 compatible cables from KBEAR and they are fantastic) and you will get as good of a sound as you will find from your source. Add a quality source (I use a Radsone EarStudio) and enjoy high quality sound on a budget.

reviews.title: Pros and Cons

Stand by time up to 150 hours.

【DESIGNED FOR SPORTS AND SIMPLE OPERATION】These mini earbuds is carefully designed according to ear geometry to ensure secure and comfort wearing.Light weight,anti-drop and sweat-proof, especially designed for sports as running,hiking,gym exercise.Simply one press to answer or reject phone calls,Bluetooth pairing,power on/off, play/pause music/video etc.Built-in Mic, great for hands-free calling, listening to music, news,or working use.

【SUPER COMPATIBILITY AND QUICK CONNECTION】 Sport headsets Compatible with all bluetooth -enabled devices; Such as iPhone,Samsung, BlackBerry, HTC, iPad Pro and any other Bluetooth-enabled equipements.Easy and qucik to connect,you will in your music world within 3 seconds.

SZHTFX provides 12 months hassle-free warranty to ensure the enjoyment of your purchase.In case of defective products, please contact SZHTFX Direct.Contact: Log in to Amazon's status--Your Orders--SZHTFX Direct --Ask a question

reviews.title: Good sound and battery life, touch activated button bad
reviews.text: Overall, this is a good earbud. However, if you hold it in your hand you run the risk of your skin/touch activating the voice commands, it hears a random snippet of what you’re saying and it calls someone from your address book that kinda sorta sounds like what you had said and you have to quickly try to hang up as your phone dials someone you haven’t talked to in 5 years, you whispering “oh f*** nononono” over and over until it hangs up and you pray it didn’t actually connect on their side but it does every damn time and then you’re forced into a conversation of pleasantries. So like...yeah it works well, good range but the touch activated button was a big no from me.

reviews.title: Can wear one at a time. Lasts a long time.
reviews.text: Absolutely adore these. Not one person looked at it in my ear and I can use one at a time. Love these!

title: BESTGOT Audio Cable with Microphone Volume Control Aux Cord 3.5mm (4.3ft / 1.3m) for PS4 Controller, Headphones, Tablet PC, Computer, Laptop,Car,Mobile Phone and More (1 Pack White)
features: Note: The microphone can only support smart phones or tablets, it can't support games, laptops or desktops. A combination of incredible sound, durability and universal compatibility make this one of the real choice for those serious about audio.Built with the same grade materials as premium headphones brands. 24K gold-plated contacts ensure the cleanest sound experience possible.

Play audio from any digital devices (phones, headphones, tablets, laptops, in-car stereos, mp3 players, portable speakers and more) other media-playing device with standard 3.5mm aux port. Hi-Fi or car stereo (fits standard 3.5mm aux ports). Plug in and play your sound.

The male-to-male stereo audio cable transmits audio in stereo format. Audio cable featuring a built-in remote mic for use with your smartphone, music player or tablet device.For Calls: Short press to answer/hang up. For Music: Press pause/play.

4.3 Feet/1.3M Double-Braided Nylon Aux Cord- Super Durable and Flexible Double-braided nylon exterior prevents tangling and adds another layer of durability, life is 5 times greater than original audio cables.
Connector of BestGot audio cable has been slightly extended to fit more devices model and phone cases. Its intelligent step-down design provides a secure fit with phones while case on.
